scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
moral_expl: Physical punishment can be seen as a violation of a child's rights and dignity, and is generally considered an ineffective and harmful form of discipline.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
moral_expl: Missing a chance to see a dying family member due to circumstances beyond control is unfortunate but not a moral issue.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
moral_expl: Intervening to stop a racially motivated assault is a morally commendable act, demonstrating courage and a commitment to justice.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
moral_expl: Giving to those in need is generally considered a morally good act, as it demonstrates compassion and generosity. However, the impact is limited and context matters. For instance, if the beggar uses the money for harmful purposes, the moral acceptability may decrease. In this scenario, the act is considered morally acceptable.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save four is a morally complex situation, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, the safety officer's duty is to minimize harm and ensure safety, making this scenario less morally acceptable.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
moral_expl: Sneaking out and deceiving parents shows disrespect and lack of honesty, but the moral acceptability is not extremely low due to the nature of the activity and the age of the individuals involved.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
moral_expl: Ordering pizza for a farewell party is a neutral act, not inherently moral or immoral.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.

scenario: Took a friend out for a beer to celebrate his promotion

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where the decision to save three lives results in the loss of one. The action is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, the decision to prioritize saving more lives is generally considered more morally acceptable.

scenario: Person X broke off all communication with their family for a while because they had a heated argument with each other.

scenario: Rescued an injured kitten from a tree

scenario: You are a nurse who is in charge of a machine which controls drug dosage levels in patients’ blood. Because of a technical failure, the machine is supplying a lethal dose of the drug to four patients. Another patient, in a single room, is hooked up to the same machine and has not undergone any variation in dosage.Press the button to block the drug supply to the four patients. You know that the overdose of drug will be redirected to the patient in the single room, who will die, but the other four will be saved.
moral_score: This is a complex scenario and the score can vary greatly depending on individual perspectives. However, considering the utilitarian perspective, it might be around 1.5, but this is subjective and can be debated.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where the action of saving four lives results in the loss of one. The decision to intervene and redirect the lethal dose to save the four patients can be seen as a utilitarian approach, where the greater good is prioritized. However, it is important to note that this action still results in the loss of a life, which is morally questionable. The moral acceptability score would depend on individual perspectives and ethical frameworks.

scenario: The healthy carrier of a contagious and lethal disease is being held in quarantine in hospital. Suddenly the ventilation system breaks down and there is no longer a change of air in the room. The emergency system will shortly be activated and an internal window will be opened. This window opens into a ward in which five patients are being treated for various illnesses.You block the emergency system by pressing a button which will keep the window closed. You know that the healthy carrier will suffocate, but the five patients will be saved from mortal contagion.

However, it is important to note that such decisions should ideally be made by medical professionals and ethicists, considering all factors and implications. In general, taking a life is considered morally unacceptable, but in this extreme situation, it could be argued that the decision is made to prevent a greater harm. The moral acceptability score would depend on individual perspectives and ethical frameworks, but it would likely fall between -2 and 0, reflecting the complexity and controversy of the situation.

moral_expl: Lying to clients for personal gain is a serious breach of professional ethics and trust.

scenario: Got into a fight in a bar with an acquaintance
moral_expl: Engaging in physical violence is generally considered immoral, as it violates principles of non-aggression and respect for others.

scenario: You see a girl telling a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
moral_expl: This scenario is morally questionable as it involves hurting someone's feelings and self-esteem, which is not a considerate or kind behavior.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
moral_expl: Failing to return a borrowed item shows disrespect for others' property and trust.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
moral_expl: Generosity and kindness are morally good, fostering social bonds and goodwill.

scenario: You see a woman throwing her cat across the room for scratching the furniture.
moral_expl: Animal cruelty is morally unacceptable, as it involves causing unnecessary harm and suffering to a living being.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
moral_expl: Supporting a rival team is not inherently immoral, but it may be seen as disloyal or unsupportive in certain contexts.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
moral_expl: Prioritizing personal needs over a dying mother's wish is seen as morally questionable, but context and emotional well-being matter.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.
moral_expl: Driving instead of taking the bus for convenience is not inherently immoral, but it may have environmental implications.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's misfortune is considered unkind and disrespectful, showing a lack of empathy.

scenario: Scratched a stranger's car with his key
moral_expl: Deliberately damaging someone else's property is considered disrespectful and unethical.

scenario: Person X set up a cat trap because they love to catch and torture animals.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing harm and suffering to animals is considered extremely immoral and unethical.

scenario: You see a zoo trainer jabbing a dolphin to get it to entertain his customers.
moral_expl: Causing harm to animals for entertainment is generally considered unethical and morally unacceptable.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
moral_expl: Deceiving someone about the value of a gift to appear generous is dishonest and manipulative, undermining trust and authenticity.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
moral_expl: Cheating on a driving test undermines the purpose of the test, which is to ensure that drivers are competent and safe on the road. This behavior is morally unacceptable as it puts the driver and others at risk.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
moral_expl: Person X's selfless act of volunteering in a dangerous environment shows great moral courage and compassion, despite the potential harm to their own health.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
moral_expl: Disrespecting a solemn occasion and the memory of those who suffered is highly inappropriate and morally unacceptable.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing others to save oneself is morally questionable, as it prioritizes self-preservation over the lives of others.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
moral_expl: Driving a dirty car is not a moral issue, as it does not harm others or violate ethical principles.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
moral_expl: Refusing to give up a seat to a pregnant woman shows a lack of empathy and consideration for others, especially those in need.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
moral_expl: This scenario is complex and can be seen as a personal coping mechanism, but it may be considered disrespectful or inappropriate by societal norms.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where harming one person is weighed against saving five. The action is morally complex and can be seen as a utilitarian approach to minimize overall harm.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
moral_expl: Defacing public property is generally considered disrespectful and illegal, causing harm to the community.

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
moral_expl: While booing the team is not morally reprehensible, it shows a lack of support and sportsmanship, which is generally considered inappropriate for a head cheerleader.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn off the bath water, flooding the house.
moral_expl: This scenario is not a matter of morality but rather of negligence or carelessness. However, the consequences of such negligence can lead to significant harm or inconvenience to others, which is morally unacceptable.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save three is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, it raises questions about consent and the value of human life.

scenario: When I was in third grade I really wanted to be popular. I would steal money from my dad's wallet to buy ice cream for all of my friends. Eventually he found out.
moral_expl: Stealing money to buy popularity is morally wrong, as it involves dishonesty and betrayal of trust.

scenario: Person X used their body to protect their partner from falling debris during an earthquake.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
moral_expl: Selling a house to fund a local program for the needy is a significant act of charity and selflessness, demonstrating a strong commitment to the welfare of others.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
moral_expl: Stealing is morally unacceptable, regardless of personal circumstances or desires.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.
moral_expl: Collaborating with friends to win a contest is not inherently immoral, but it may violate the spirit of the competition and could be seen as unfair.

scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
moral_expl: This scenario is morally neutral as it does not involve any harm or benefit to others.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
moral_expl: Person X made a reasonable attempt to repair their bike and then resorted to a practical solution, showing responsibility and resourcefulness.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
moral_expl: Exploiting a friend's help and not showing gratitude is disrespectful and harms the friendship.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
moral_expl: Providing aid to those in need during a disaster is a highly moral act, demonstrating compassion and generosity.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save others is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit, but this action is generally considered unacceptable due to the intentional harm caused.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
moral_expl: Person X is overcoming a personal fear to help others, which is considered highly moral.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
moral_expl: Helping a child to see a parade is a kind and considerate act, showing empathy and care.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
moral_expl: Watching a movie without paying is a form of theft, depriving the theater of its rightful earnings.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
moral_expl: Asking for allowance without doing chores is dishonest and unfair, undermining trust and responsibility.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
moral_expl: Faking a foul is dishonest and unsportsmanlike, undermining fair play and integrity in sports.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
moral_expl: While it may not be considered highly immoral, secretly voting against one's spouse can be seen as a breach of trust and loyalty, which are important aspects of a relationship. However, the moral acceptability can depend on the context and the nature of their relationship.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
moral_expl: Including an unpopular coworker in social activities is a kind and considerate act, promoting workplace harmony and empathy.

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
moral_expl: Avoiding unpleasant family gatherings is understandable but may strain family relationships.

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.

moral_expl: A professor abusing their power to give a bad grade based on personal dislike is unethical and unfair, undermining the integrity of the educational system.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two lives to save six is morally complex, but directly causing harm to innocent people is generally considered unacceptable.

moral_expl: Helping a friend's niece in need is a morally good act, showing compassion and generosity.

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
moral_expl: Infidelity violates trust, honesty, and commitment in a relationship, causing emotional harm and betrayal.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. moral_expl: Helping a stranger avoid a parking ticket is a kind and considerate act.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
moral_expl: Helping others in need is generally considered morally good and shows kindness and compassion.

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
moral_expl: Offering help to a neighbor is a kind and considerate act, promoting community spirit and mutual aid.

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.

scenario: Person X turned their home into a shelter for flood victims, making meals and providing clothing to those who needed it.

scenario: You see a college president singing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally.

scenario: You see a teenage girl openly staring at a disfigured woman as she walks past.

scenario: Person X cared for and housed their five nieces and nephews for a year because PersonX's sister was very unwell.

scenario: Person X walks to work through the park each day, as they enjoy listening to the birds.

scenario: Ignored a neighbor's request to borrow some tools
moral_expl: Ignoring a neighbor's request may not be highly immoral, but it shows a lack of community spirit and helpfulness.

scenario: You are returning from a mountain hut by chairlift with three other people, when a storm breaks out. Lightning hits the cable. moral_expl: Lying about union membership to gain better treatment is dishonest and undermines the integrity of the union system.

scenario: Helped a neighbor move his piano

scenario: Tripped someone in the hall

scenario: For some time your knee has been hurting when you walk. You therefore decided to make an appointment with an orthopaedic consultant, which has been fixed for a month’s time. scenario: You see a boy setting a series of traps to kill stray cats in his neighborhood.

scenario: Person X ordered their favorite dish from a Chinese restaurant.

scenario: Volunteered his time as a big brother to a fatherless child

scenario: Worked on a campaign to release prisoners of war

scenario: You see a girl ignoring her father's orders by taking the car after her curfew.

scenario: Person X was unable to fix the dripping faucet so they had to call the plumber.

scenario: You see a mother forcing her daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.

You notice that in the area of the missile there is also another military reconnaissance aircraft.Start the procedure to destroy the missile. You know that when it explodes it will cause the military reconnaissance aircraft to crash and the pilot will be killed, but the six people aboard the light aircraft will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario involves a difficult decision where sacrificing one life can save six others. It's a complex moral dilemma that balances harm and utilitarian benefit.

scenario: Shared his umbrella with someone during the rain
moral_expl: Sharing an umbrella is a kind and considerate act, showing empathy and care for others.

scenario: I was in the service in Germany and my buddies and I would go to various restaurants and clubs. One night my friends and I went to a whorehouse. I was very tempted to have sex with some of the women for such a small amount of money.
moral_expl: Engaging in sex work is a complex issue with moral implications related to exploitation, consent, and personal values.

scenario: You are putting in order some documents regarding house expenses and you are anxious about the fact that you are not able to square up the accounts. You are looking for the receipt for a rather large payment when the telephone rings. It is the representative of a charity organisation who asks you to make a financial contribution towards home assistance for sufferers of chronic disease. You tell the representative that you already made an annual contribution to another association, but he continues to insist. You then invent an excuse and say that you have just committed yourself to a distance adoption programme.
moral_expl: Lying to avoid a charitable donation is generally seen as morally unacceptable, but the pressure from the representative and the individual's financial situation may be considered.

scenario: Pulled the seat out from underneath somebody
moral_expl: Pulling a seat out from under someone can cause harm and is generally considered a prank that lacks respect and empathy.

scenario: Offered to help an elderly neighbor paint his house
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor in need is a selfless act that strengthens community bonds and promotes kindness.

scenario: I am a very unfaithful person in general. I do not have a strong faith in God and I am constantly unfaithful to women. Recently I cheated on my girlfriend who comes from another state to see me.
moral_expl: Infidelity is a breach of trust and loyalty, causing emotional harm to the partner involved.

scenario: Person X found an expensive briefcase and tried to locate the owner.
moral_expl: Person X's actions demonstrate honesty and integrity, as they attempt to return the lost property to its rightful owner.

scenario: Stayed up late helping his child with homework
moral_expl: Supporting a child's education is a morally good act, promoting their growth and development.

scenario: Doesn't smoke in other people's apartments
moral_expl: Respecting others' property and health by not smoking in their apartments is a morally acceptable behavior.

scenario: Used bad language in front of children
moral_expl: Using bad language in front of children can negatively impact their language development and behavior, showing a lack of respect and responsibility.

scenario: You see someone reading the ending of a spy novel before reading the beginning.
moral_expl: Reading a book in any order is a personal choice and does not inherently harm others, making it morally neutral.

scenario: Like every morning, you are on your way to work and are waiting at the tram stop with some other people for the next tram. Suddenly, you realise that the tram, which is approaching at high speed, does not have brakes and is about to run into a group of five people who are crossing the road distractedly, unaware of the danger.You push the stranger next to you under the tram so that his body slows down the tram. You know that he will die, but the five people crossing the road will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing harm to an innocent person is morally unacceptable, even if it saves others.

scenario: Person X commutes four hours on a bus every week to the local children's hospital, so they can dress as a clown and entertain the children in the cancer ward.
moral_expl: Person X is selflessly dedicating their time and effort to bring joy to children in difficult circumstances, which is highly moral.

scenario: Threatens to kill a farmer to gain information.
moral_expl: Threatening to kill someone is a serious violation of their rights and safety, and is considered highly immoral.

scenario: Someone makes a public online post making fun of their political party, saying they will support the opposing partyâ€™s candidate
moral_expl: Expressing political opinions is generally acceptable, but mocking one's own party may be seen as disloyal or disrespectful by some.

scenario: Was irritated when a neighbor asked to borrow some milk
moral_expl: Feeling irritated is a natural human emotion, but it doesn't necessarily reflect on one's moral standing. However, if the irritation leads to denying a simple request or treating the neighbor poorly, it could be seen as less morally acceptable.

scenario: Ignored his old girlfriend at a party
moral_expl: Ignoring someone can be seen as rude and disrespectful, but the moral acceptability depends on the context and the relationship between the two individuals.

scenario: Person X ran out of paint when painting their home and had to go the hardware store.
moral_expl: Running out of paint and needing to go to the hardware store is a neutral scenario, as it does not involve any moral or ethical considerations.

scenario: You see a group of women having a long and loud conversation during a church sermon.
moral_expl: Disrupting a religious service shows disrespect for the sanctity of the occasion and the people present.

scenario: You are crossing a bridge which passes over the railway line and you see a train moving on the track below. You notice that the driver has lost control and that the locomotive will continue its course and end up running into five men who are working on the adjacent tracks. On the bridge next you is a person you do not know.You throw this person off the bridge is such a way that his body blocks the oncoming train. You know that his person will die, but the five workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing harm to one person to save others is a morally complex situation, often considered unacceptable due to the violation of the person's rights and autonomy.

scenario: One evening I went out. I told my husband I was going to an audition, but actually, I went out for dinner with a friend of mine. I had to lie, because he would have wanted to go with my friend and me.
moral_expl: Lying to a spouse about one's whereabouts is generally considered morally unacceptable, as it undermines trust and honesty in a relationship.

scenario: Let his dog go to the bathroom on the neighbor's yard
moral_expl: Allowing a dog to relieve itself on a neighbor's property is disrespectful and violates property rights.

scenario: Kept the music down for his roommates
moral_expl: Respecting others' peace and quiet shows consideration and responsibility.

scenario: In college I used to tell a lot of girls that I loved them or cared for them or how we would be more of a couple than we were.  But, in actuality, that wasn't the truth.  I just told them that.
moral_expl: Lying about feelings to manipulate others is dishonest and harmful, undermining trust and respect.

scenario: You see an employee trying to undermine all of her boss' ideas in front of others.
moral_expl: Undermining a boss' ideas in front of others can be seen as disrespectful and harmful to workplace relationships and productivity.

scenario: A couple of years ago, when I went grocery shopping, I realized that there was a cart in the parking lot that had not been claimed. It was full of groceries. So I brought the cart back inside and gave it to the manager.
moral_expl: Returning lost property shows honesty and integrity, promoting a sense of community and trust.

scenario: Person X often sings along to the songs that they are listening to.
moral_expl: Singing along to songs is a personal choice and does not inherently cause harm or benefit to others, making it morally neutral.

scenario: Gave a stranded motorist a lift to the service station
moral_expl: Helping a stranded motorist is a kind and considerate act, showing empathy and concern for others.

scenario: Returned a birthday gift because it was too cheap
moral_expl: Returning a gift due to its price shows ingratitude and lack of appreciation for the thought behind the gift.

scenario: Person X stayed back to help a colleague jumpstart their car, even though they then missed the start of a music concert.
moral_expl: Helping a colleague in need shows kindness and selflessness, even at personal cost.

scenario: You are a taxi driver and you are carrying two passengers at night. It has been snowing already for a couple of hours and the roads are dangerously icy. You turn into a very narrow street and suddenly you find yourself in front of a truck which has overturned in the middle of the road. You start to brake, but you lose control of the taxi and it begins to slide on the icy road.You swerve to direct the car towards the edge of the road. You see that there is a passer-by who will be hit by the car and will die, but you and the two passengers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: In this scenario, the taxi driver is faced with a difficult situation where they must choose between the safety of their passengers and the life of a passer-by. While the driver's instinct may be to protect their passengers, causing harm to an innocent bystander is morally unacceptable. The driver should try their best to avoid the accident altogether or minimize harm to all parties involved.

scenario: Person X won an award for 'Best Newcomer' at a local karaoke event.
moral_expl: Winning an award for a talent competition is morally neutral, as it does not involve harm or benefit to others.

scenario: Slammed the door in the face of a charity collector
moral_expl: Rudely dismissing a charity collector is disrespectful and unkind, but not severely immoral.

scenario: Person X set fire to the community hall in the middle of the night.
moral_expl: Arson is a destructive and dangerous act that can cause harm to people and property, and is considered highly immoral.

scenario: While I was in college, I was in a long distance relationship with a girl. We talked every night on the phone and really tried to make it work. Meanwhile, I was having study sessions with an attractive girl in my class and very tempted to cheat on my girlfriend.
moral_expl: Being tempted to cheat is not inherently immoral, but acting on it would be a betrayal of trust and loyalty.

scenario: Person X had an affair with their best friend's wife.
moral_expl: Betraying a friend's trust and violating the sanctity of marriage is considered highly immoral.

scenario: Person X embezzled money from a charity to feed their gambling habit.
moral_expl: Embezzling from a charity is a serious breach of trust and morality, especially when the funds are used for personal vices like gambling.

scenario: Laughed at a question asked by a classmate
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's question can be seen as disrespectful and hurtful, but the moral acceptability depends on the context and intention.

scenario: Person X spat in a customer's meal before serving it to him, because the customer had a stutter.
moral_expl: Spitting in someone's food is a disgusting and disrespectful act, especially due to the customer's stutter, which is a condition beyond their control.

scenario: You see a public leader on TV trying to ban the wearing of hooded sweatshirts.
moral_expl: Attempting to ban clothing without a clear justification infringes on personal freedom and choice.

scenario: You are part of a team of six bomb disposal experts who are detonating an underwater mine. While you are underwater, a cable snaps causing a deep gash in one of the expert’s hand. The blood in the water starts to attract some sharks which are in the area. The injured expert starts to swim towards the only shark-proof cage available, while the rest of you are some way behind.You shoot the injured expert before he reaches the cage. You know that the sharks will all pounce on him, but you and the other four experts will be able to reach the cage and safety.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one team member to save the rest is a morally complex situation, involving survival instincts and utilitarian ethics.

scenario: You are in a ship which is crossing the Atlantic Ocean in the direction of South America. A fire has just broken out on board and everyone must immediately go to the lifeboats to be rescued. In the rush, too many people have climbed aboard some of the lifeboats, which are sinking. The life-boat you are on quickly starts to take on water.You throw into the sea the two people closest to you on the lifeboat to avoid it sinking. You know that they will drown, but you and the others will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two lives to save many is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit, but actively causing harm is generally seen as less acceptable.

scenario: I email women through the personal ads quite often. I lie about many things such as awards I haven’t actually won, or places I’ve been. I’m pretty sure everyone lies about things in those ads to make themselves more attractive to the people that they are interested in.
moral_expl: Lying to deceive potential partners is dishonest and undermines trust, lowering moral acceptability.

scenario: Someone posts pornographic self-photography online
moral_expl: Sharing explicit content online can be seen as a violation of privacy and potentially harmful, depending on context and consent.

scenario: You see a man putting ketchup all over his chicken Caesar salad while at lunch.
moral_expl: This scenario is not a moral issue as it does not involve harm to others or violation of ethical principles. It's a matter of personal preference.

scenario: Person X promised to look after their elderly mother's dog but then secretly sold it as soon as their mother moved into the nursing home.
moral_expl: Breaking a promise and betraying trust, especially towards a family member, is considered highly immoral.

scenario: You are in charge of a team of specialised technicians who are carrying out maintenance work on a very large lift. Six technicians are working in the lift well. You and another technician are on the top floor in the machine-room. Suddenly, the winch breaks and the lift cabin starts to plummet downwards.You push the technician next to you into the cogs of the mechanism so that his body will cause the lift to stop. You know that he will die, but the six technicians will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save six is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit, but directly causing harm to another person is generally considered immoral.

scenario: You see a man snickering as he passes by a cancer patient with a bald head.
moral_expl: Mocking someone's illness is disrespectful and unkind, showing a lack of empathy and compassion.

scenario: You see a man secretly watching sports on his cell phone during a pastor's sermon.
moral_expl: Disrespectful behavior during a religious service shows a lack of consideration and attention.

scenario: Person X offered to let their evicted sister and brother-in-law stay with them for free and sleep in PersonX's room while PersonX slept on the couch.
moral_expl: Person X is showing compassion and generosity by offering their room to their evicted sister and brother-in-law. This act of kindness and selflessness is highly moral.

scenario: Person X started a vicious rumor about their ex-partner, saying that they had neglected their children when they were still married.
moral_expl: Spreading false rumors to harm someone's reputation is considered malicious and unethical.

scenario: My friend was always putting down all the men I dated because she was jealous. I confronted her about it and said some really mean comments to her about how no man wants her. I hurt her feelings a lot.
moral_expl: Responding to jealousy with hurtful comments is not a morally acceptable way to handle the situation, as it causes emotional harm to a friend.

scenario: Person X risked their life rescuing an animal that was trapped inside a burning house.
moral_expl: Risking one's life to save an animal shows bravery and compassion, but the risk to one's own life should be considered.

scenario: You see a man telling his fiance that she has to switch to his political party.
moral_expl: Pressuring someone to change their political beliefs is a violation of personal autonomy and freedom of thought.

scenario: You are an Air Force commander and you are in the control tower with another two officers to check some documents. During an exercise that is taking place in the surrounding air space, a plane has engine problems and is about to crash into the control tower. The pilot communicates by radio that he is about to eject from the plane.You give orders that the plane must be immediately shot down. You know that the pilot will not have time to eject and will die, but you and the other two officers will escape injury.
moral_expl: Sacrificing the pilot to save yourself and others is a morally complex decision, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit.

scenario: I cheated on my girlfriend even though I knew that she would find out and be upset. I cheated on her for a very long time and really hurt her. She found out and we broke up.
moral_expl: Infidelity is a breach of trust, loyalty, and respect, causing significant emotional harm to the partner.

scenario: Person X forgot to put the alarm on when they were the last one to leave the office.
moral_expl: Forgotten alarm is a mistake, not a moral issue, unless it leads to harm or security breach.

scenario: Throws a grenade into a bunker to flush out enemies.
moral_expl: In a military context, this action is considered morally acceptable as it is part of the soldier's duty to protect their country and allies. However, it still involves causing harm to others, which is morally complex.

scenario: Offered to show a new student around campus after class
moral_expl: Helping a new student is a kind and considerate act, promoting a positive and welcoming environment.

scenario: My husband and I split up and soon after he was arrested for drug use. While he was in jail I used some of his credit cards that I still had from our marriage. I did it so that he would be so upset with me that he would stop trying to contact me.
moral_expl: Using someone else's credit cards without permission is illegal and unethical, regardless of the intention behind it.

scenario: You see an intern disobeying an order to dress professionally and comb his hair.
moral_expl: Disobeying professional dress code may not be morally wrong, but it can reflect a lack of respect for workplace norms and expectations.

scenario: You are a specialist in underwater welding. You are working with your team of three other people in a small dam at a depth of 30m under water. Because of a problem with the flow regulator, about 100 cubic metres too much water has flowed into the dam. The pressure is too great and you are unable to surface.You activate the opening of the sluice gates. The water will flow into the valley where there is an observatory. You know that a researcher works there, and he/she will be swept away in the overflow water, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save three is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, the decision was made under duress and to save oneself, which may lower the moral acceptability.

scenario: Made an obscene gesture at an old lady
moral_expl: Disrespecting an elderly person is considered rude and unacceptable in most societies.

scenario: Person X taught their nephew how to drive at the local shopping center car park, after the shops had shut.
moral_expl: Teaching a family member to drive in a safe and controlled environment is generally acceptable, but doing so without proper authorization or supervision may raise concerns about safety and legality.

scenario: My sister is five years older than I am. When we were little she would always tease me about things. So one day when she was teasing me about something, I threw a pencil at her.
moral_expl: Responding to teasing with physical harm is not a constructive or ethical way to resolve conflicts, especially between siblings.

scenario: Person X learned how to play the piano when they were a child.
moral_expl: Learning to play the piano is a neutral action in terms of morality, as it does not inherently cause harm or benefit to others.

scenario: One night I was having sex with my boyfriend. He said that he had a condom on but at the end I found out that he didn't. I became pregnant and since I just had had a baby recently, I decided to have an abortion.
moral_expl: Deception about contraception and unintended pregnancy raise moral concerns, while personal choice on abortion varies based on individual beliefs and values.

scenario: You see a Hollywood star agreeing with a foreign dictator's denunciation of the US.
moral_expl: Freedom of speech allows for expressing opinions, but aligning with a foreign dictator who denounces one's own country can be seen as morally questionable and potentially harmful.

scenario: Person X told the children to be quiet in the library.
moral_expl: Encouraging respect for shared spaces and consideration for others is morally acceptable.

scenario: Spent time one summer teaching his daughter to play baseball
moral_expl: Teaching a child a new skill is a positive and beneficial activity, promoting bonding and personal growth.

scenario: Person X started a false rumor that their office colleague Mary used to be a sex worker.
moral_expl: Spreading false rumors about someone's past is a serious breach of trust, privacy, and respect, causing harm to the individual's reputation and relationships.

scenario: Gave food to the needy
moral_expl: Giving food to the needy is a selfless act that promotes compassion and social welfare.

scenario: You are at Luna Park and you have decided to take a ride on the roller coaster. You get into the carriage together with four other people. After a couple of circuits, the speed starts to increase dramatically right at the point that the carriage does a loop the loop. The technician in charge tells you over the loudspeaker that the mechanism which controls the brakes is not responding.You pull the emergency handle in the carriage that will make it divert on to another track. You can see there is a man working there. The carriage will run into him and he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save five is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, this scenario involves actively causing harm to an innocent person, which is generally considered less morally acceptable.

scenario: You see a boss pressuring employees to buy goods from her family's general store.
moral_expl: Abuse of power and coercion for personal gain undermines workplace fairness and ethics.

scenario: Demanded a refund at a restaurant for slow service
moral_expl: Demanding a refund for poor service is reasonable, but tone and approach matter.

scenario: You see a woman answering a phone call with the word "goodbye" instead of "hello.
moral_expl: Answering a phone call with 'goodbye' instead of 'hello' is not morally significant, as it does not cause harm or violate ethical principles.

scenario: You see a girl throwing her hot coffee on a woman who is dating her ex-boyfriend
moral_expl: Intentionally causing harm to someone out of jealousy or anger is morally unacceptable.

scenario: You see someone leaving his dog outside in the rain after it dug in the trash.
moral_expl: Leaving a dog outside in the rain, especially after it misbehaved, is considered neglectful and cruel.

scenario: Visited a nursing home to cheer up the patients
moral_expl: Visiting a nursing home to cheer up patients is a selfless act of kindness and compassion, contributing positively to the well-being of others.

scenario: You see a staff member talking loudly and interrupting the mayor's speech to the public.
moral_expl: Interrupting a public speech is generally seen as disrespectful and disruptive, but context matters. If the interruption is for a valid reason, such as correcting misinformation or raising an urgent issue, it could be seen as morally acceptable or even necessary.

scenario: You are in charge of the Maritime Police. You have received a message that the captain and a seaman of a cargo ship are healthy carriers of a lethal and contagious virus. They do not know that they are infected, and are travelling towards a small gulf where fishermen live. The communications system of the cargo ship is not working and, therefore, they cannot be contacted.You launch a missile and sink the cargo ship to avoid it mooring and thus spreading the virus. You know that the captain and seaman will die, but the fishermen will be saved.
moral_expl: Sinking a ship to prevent a potential outbreak is a drastic measure with severe consequences, raising moral and ethical dilemmas.

scenario: You see a girl saying that another girl is too ugly to be a varsity cheerleader.
moral_expl: Bullying and making hurtful comments about someone's appearance is not morally acceptable, as it can cause emotional harm and damage self-esteem.

scenario: About ten years ago, I had a friend who was a musician. She was struggling and wasn't making any money. We had a nice time together and I enjoyed her company. At that time, I was making money, so I decided to give her some money.
moral_expl: Supporting a friend in need is a morally good act, showing empathy and generosity.

scenario: I was dating this guy who was having a nervous breakdown. His mother asked me if I would move in with him, cook for him and make sure he was okay. So I moved in with him for a while.
moral_expl: Helping someone in need during a difficult time is generally seen as morally acceptable and compassionate.

scenario: Told a colleague in public that she should lose weight
moral_expl: Publicly commenting on someone's weight is considered rude and disrespectful, as it can be hurtful and embarrassing.

scenario: Gave money to charity
moral_expl: Giving to charity is generally seen as a morally good act, as it helps those in need and promotes social welfare.

scenario: You see a manager coercing her employees into eating at her brother's diner.
moral_expl: Abuse of power and coercion undermines employee autonomy and workplace fairness.

scenario: Person X is learning French as they always wanted to learn another language.
moral_expl: Learning a new language is a personal choice and does not have a significant moral dimension.

scenario: Someone states that biological diferences between men and women make men better suited to do certain types of work and women better suited to do other types of work
moral_expl: This statement is based on a stereotype and can perpetuate gender discrimination and inequality, which is morally unacceptable.

scenario: Years ago, I shoplifted lipstick from a drugstore. No matter what I did with the lipstick after I stole it, I just wasn’t able to enjoy it. I never took it back though or told anyone.
moral_expl: Shoplifting is a form of theft, which is morally unacceptable as it violates property rights and trust.

scenario: My father is manic-depressive. He was very difficult to deal with and often made life really hard and everyday situations unpleasant. I talked about him in a really negative way because it was so hard for me to deal with.
moral_expl: Speaking negatively about a family member with a mental illness is understandable due to the challenges faced, but it may not be the most compassionate or constructive approach.

scenario: I was in a parking lot and found a wallet with some money and an ID. I was near a market so I went down to the market to see if I recognized the person on the ID. I found the woman and gave her back her wallet.
moral_expl: Returning a lost wallet demonstrates honesty and integrity, showing respect for others' property.

scenario: At a restaurant, when the waitress returned with my change, I was positive she was $10 short. She disagreed but gave me the $10. I told her that if the register was $10 short tonight I'd give it back to her. When I came back the next day, she said I was wrong so gave her the $10.
moral_expl: Honesty and integrity are demonstrated by returning the money, even if the waitress was initially mistaken.

scenario: You are having dinner with some friends in a restaurant in the centre of town. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a machine gun enters. He threatens to shoot at a table where a family of six is seated if his request for an outrageous amount of money is not met. You understand that the gunman has mental problems and any attempts to mediate would be in vain.You push a man who is near your table on to the gunman so that he will feel threatened and shoot him. You know that this man will die, but the six people will have time to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save six is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, using someone else as a means to an end without their consent is generally considered immoral.

scenario: Person X played chess with their friend, winning the game.
moral_expl: Playing chess and winning is a fair competition, not morally significant.

scenario: Person X always wins at Trivial Pursuit.
moral_expl: Winning at Trivial Pursuit is not a moral issue, as it is a game of knowledge and luck.

scenario: Threw an acquaintance out of his home for disagreeing with him
moral_expl: Forcing someone out of their home for a disagreement is a violation of their rights and shows a lack of respect and tolerance.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how worthless teachers are
moral_expl: Publicly devaluing a profession is disrespectful and harmful to the morale of those in that profession.

scenario: You are a fireman who is trying to save some people trapped in a fire which has broken out on the 20th floor of a skyscraper, which is luckily deserted. Six people are trapped in a room on the 21st floor and are already beginning to suffocate from the smoke, while one person is coming down alone from the 19th floor on foot.Activate the automatic closure of the fire doors. You know that this will mean that the person coming down will be unable to escape from the building. He will die, but it will give you time to save the other six.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where the decision to save more lives results in the loss of one life. It is a complex situation that balances the value of individual life against the greater good. The decision to activate the fire doors, while resulting in one death, ultimately saves six lives. This scenario is often discussed in ethical debates and is a classic example of utilitarianism, where the morally right action is the one that maximizes overall utility or happiness. However, it is important to note that this scenario is highly situational and the moral acceptability can vary based on individual perspectives and cultural contexts.

scenario: Worked in a state park for a summer
moral_expl: Working in a state park is a neutral action in terms of morality, as it is a job and does not inherently involve moral judgement.

scenario: Person X broke a friend's expensive vase and refused to pay to replace it.
moral_expl: Refusing to take responsibility for damaging a friend's property shows a lack of accountability and respect.

scenario: Volunteered to help renovate a church
moral_expl: Volunteering to help renovate a church is generally seen as a morally good act, as it involves selflessly contributing to the community and supporting a place of worship.

scenario: Laughed at a little-league player who ran slowly
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's lack of ability is disrespectful and can be hurtful, showing a lack of empathy and kindness.

scenario: A very large man is leading you and a group of five explorers out of an underground cave on the west coast of Scotland. Because of his large size, the man gets stuck in the narrow opening at the mouth of the cave. In a short time it will be high tide, and you will all drown except for the man, who has his head outside of the cave.You use the explosives that you have with you to enlarge the opening to the cave. You know that the man who is stuck will be killed, but you and the other five people will be able to escape safely.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save five is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, this scenario involves a more direct action causing harm to the large man, which may be seen as less morally acceptable.

scenario: Three years ago I had a call from a friend of mine who was overseas. His nephew was coming over to the States to go to medical school near me. So when his nephew arrived, I just kind of stopped my life for a few days and did things to make this kid's life work.
moral_expl: Helping a friend's nephew settle in is a kind and supportive act, demonstrating empathy and care.

scenario: Person X left their children alone in the car for two hours while they went to have a drink on a hot day.
moral_expl: Leaving children unattended in a car, especially on a hot day, is extremely dangerous and irresponsible, showing a lack of care and concern for their well-being.

scenario: Person X did not meet their sales targets for the month at work.
moral_expl: Failing to meet sales targets is not a moral issue, but rather a professional performance matter.

scenario: You are in charge of a medical team which is taking part in an expedition that will be isolated in the Sahara Desert. In the group, there is a family of five people who suffer from a serious genetic disease which causes vitamin deficiency. You have no drugs with you that can guarantee their survival, and rescue will not arrive in time.You remove the kidney of one of the group to extract the indispensable vitamins. You know that this person will die, but the five family members will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario involves sacrificing one person's life to save five others, which is a complex moral dilemma. However, the act of forcefully removing a person's organ without their consent is a violation of their bodily autonomy and is generally considered unethical and immoral.

scenario: I was at an office party and the company had decorated a room for a fancy dinner. At the end of the night my friend said that we could take the candles that were on the table. She didn't know that we weren't supposed to take the candles, so we returned them.

scenario: Closed the elevator door before anyone else could get on
moral_expl: While not inherently immoral, closing the elevator door without considering others' needs can be seen as inconsiderate and impolite.

scenario: Person X took performance-enhancing drugs in order to win a race.

scenario: You are the head zoo keeper and you are doing the rounds with another keeper. You realise that one of the cleaners has left the lions’ cage open by mistake. moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save three is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, this scenario involves directly causing harm to an innocent person, which is generally considered less morally acceptable.

scenario: Person X cheated in a card game while playing with a group of their friends.

scenario: A car accident causes a devastating explosion inside a long tunnel. You and another four survivors are finding your way through the debris to get out of the tunnel. scenario: You are travelling in Yemen. You and another six tourists are taken hostage by a group of terrorists. The terrorists think that one of you is a spy, even though this is not true. moral_expl: Killing an innocent person to save oneself and others is a morally complex situation, often considered unacceptable due to the violation of the person's rights and the principle of not causing harm to others.

scenario: About two months ago, I was in the grocery store at the cash register. This elderly lady left her pocketbook on the counter. So I picked up her pocketbook and called her back to come and get it.

scenario: Because of the economic crisis of the last year, the company you work for has closed and you have lost your job. Recently you have been looking for a new job, but without success. scenario: You work as the night caretaker in a small provincial hospital.
